text: [@file:#1:CUTTER CANADA]Build me a pre production planning system that we can fill in.As we break down the script
I'll help you create a pre-production planning system for your CUTTER CANADA project. Let me first analyze the script to understand what needs to be tracked.

data.analyse_result:
The provided document is a pilot script for a project titled 'CUTTER' (image_1), set in Newfoundland, Canada. The story follows a protagonist named Cutter (a.k.a. Jack), a man with a mysterious and troubled past in Montreal (image_46), who has fled to Fogo Island. The production elements identified for pre-production planning are as follows:

**1. Key Locations:**
- **Exteriors:** Trans-Canada Highway (Montreal to Newfoundland), Fogo Island ferry, and the rugged coastline of Joe Batt’s Arm (image_3, image_58).
- **Interiors:** Auntie Milli’s Bird Observatory (image_3), the RCMP Outpost (including an interrogation room and bullpen) (image_8, image_11), The Wreckhouse bar (image_41), the Fogo Island Inn (Plush Room and Adjoining Room) (image_37, image_57), and a dilapidated beach rental house (image_31).

**2. Characters:**
- **Cutter:** A mid-thirties man with facial injuries, hiding a past mistake (image_2).
- **Inspector Abby White:** A local plainclothes RCMP officer (image_5).
- **Goose:** Cutter’s ex-brother-in-law, a local who micro-doses psilocybin and runs a unique shop called 'The Berg Baron' (image_15, image_46).
- **The Ross Family:** Fred (a wealthy, ruthless real estate developer), Eric (his son), and Nikki (Eric's kidnapped wife) (image_17, image_21, image_36).
- **Supporting Locals:** Biscuit (an eccentric owner of a polar bear), Captain Tony (barkeep), and Rory White (Abby's father) (image_5, image_41, image_34).

**3. Props and Animals:**
- **Animals:** Sarge, an 8-foot-6-inch polar bear (image_5, image_51); over 100 rescue birds, including parrots that mimic dialogue (image_3, image_4); Pirate, a Nova Scotia Duck Tolling Retriever (image_29); and a scrappy red fox (image_24).
- **Vehicles:** A 'beat-to-hell' Buick packed with belongings (image_2), a vintage Vespa (image_19), and an RCMP cruiser.
- **Production Props:** A briefcase filled with cash and paper 'blanks' (image_57, image_60), a surveillance van with a 'Tim Hortons Vanilla Dip' air freshener (image_16, image_58), an iceberg-melting rig (image_46), and a variety of local-themed decor (cod-shaped mailbox, cod traps) (image_28).

**4. Key Production Sequences:**
- **Stunt/Action:** A polar bear containment scene (image_5, image_6), a physical altercation in the bird observatory (image_4), a high-speed U-turn (image_28), and a climactic shooting on a cliffside trail (image_59).

text: Perfect! Now I'll create a comprehensive pre-production planning system tailored to your CUTTER CANADA script. This will be a fillable document that you can use as you break down each scene.

text:
Perfect! I've created a comprehensive pre-production planning system for CUTTER CANADA. The system includes:

## **What's Included:**

✅ **Project Overview Header** - Key production info at a glance  
✅ **Master Scene Breakdown Sheet** - Detailed tracking for every scene with columns for cast, props, vehicles, animals, stunts, and more  
✅ **Location Breakdown** - All 9+ locations organized by INT/EXT with permit tracking  
✅ **Character/Cast Tracking** - All principal characters with fitting and call time fields  
✅ **Animal Wrangling Schedule** - Dedicated section for Sarge (polar bear), 100+ birds, Pirate (dog), and the fox  
✅ **Props Master List** - Organized by category including the cash briefcase, iceberg rig, vehicles  
✅ **Stunt/Action Coordination** - Safety protocols for the 4 major action sequences  
✅ **Daily Production Schedule Template** - Fillable day-by-day shooting plan

## **How to Use:**
The system is designed to be filled in progressively as you break down the script scene-by-scene. Each section has fillable fields, checkboxes, and space for notes. You can access the **interactive HTML version** (link provided above) to fill it digitally, or print it for physical production binders.

The color-coding helps you quickly identify: Locations (Blue), Cast/Animals (Green), Props/Vehicles (Yellow), and Stunts/Safety (Red).

```sug
<suggestion>
  <label>Add Call Sheets</label>
  <prompt>Create daily call sheet templates for CUTTER CANADA production</prompt>
</suggestion>
<suggestion>
  <label>Budget Breakdown</label>
  <prompt>Generate a budget breakdown template for the CUTTER CANADA pilot</prompt>
</suggestion>
<suggestion>
  <label>Shot List Template</label>
  <prompt>Create a detailed shot list template for CUTTER CANADA scenes</prompt>
</suggestion>
```

text:
Perfect! I've created all three additional production documents for CUTTER CANADA:

## **📋 Complete Pre-Production Package:**

### **1. Daily Call Sheet Template**
Professional call sheet with crew/cast call times, scene schedule, location details, equipment checklist, and important contacts. Ready to fill in for each shoot day.

### **2. Budget Breakdown Template**
Comprehensive budget document covering:
- Above the Line (Rights, Producer, Director, Cast)
- Below the Line (All departments from Camera to Post-Production)
- Special sections for Animal Wrangling, Stunts, and unique props
- Line items for all 9+ locations
- 10% Contingency built in

### **3. Shot List Template**
Detailed shot-by-shot breakdown with:
- Technical specs (shot size, angle, movement, lens)
- Special sections for the 4 major action sequences (polar bear, bird observatory fight, U-turn, cliffside shooting)
- Safety protocol checkboxes
- Equipment and lighting notes
- Space for storyboard thumbnails

## **Your Complete System:**
You now have a full pre-production suite that covers planning, scheduling, budgeting, and shot execution. All documents are fillable and can be accessed via the HTML links for digital use or printed for production binders.

text:
Perfect! I've created a complete suite of **visual reference templates** for CUTTER CANADA. Here's your comprehensive visual department package:

## **🎨 Visual Reference Templates Created:**

### **1. Visual Style Guide**
Complete cinematography and visual tone reference including:
- Color palettes (Atlantic blues, coastal greys, weathered browns)
- Lighting approach (natural coastal light, moody interiors)
- Camera style and movement
- Location-specific visual references
- Film/photography influences

### **2. Costume Design Bible**
Character-by-character costume breakdowns with:
- Cutter's travel-worn look & facial injuries
- Inspector Abby's RCMP uniform variations
- Goose's quirky local style
- Ross family's wealth indicators
- Scene-by-scene costume continuity tracking
- Fabric, texture, and aging notes

### **3. Props Master Reference**
Comprehensive props documentation:
- Hero props (cash briefcase, iceberg rig, Tim Hortons air freshener, cod mailbox)
- Weapons & safety props
- Personal props by character
- Location-specific props (bird observatory, bar, RCMP outpost)
- Vehicle interior props
- Acquisition and tracking systems

### **4. Production Design Lookbook**
Location-by-location design breakdowns:
- Bird Observatory (cluttered, eccentric)
- RCMP Outpost (institutional, cold)
- Wreckhouse Bar (warm dive)
- Fogo Island Inn (modern luxury)
- Beach House (weathered decay)
- Color palettes, mood boards, floor plans, set dressing lists

### **5. Hair & Makeup Design Reference**
Character appearance tracking:
- Cutter's facial injury progression (special effects makeup)
- Professional vs. personal looks for all characters
- Nikki's degradation timeline (kidnapping)
- Newfoundland climate considerations
- Continuity tracking charts
- Product lists and application times

### **6. Vehicle & Transportation Reference**
Picture vehicle documentation:
- Cutter's beat-up Buick (with stunt double specs)
- Vintage Vespa
- RCMP Cruiser (authentic markings)
- Surveillance Van (with Tim Hortons air freshener)
- Stunt requirements for U-turn sequence
- Multi-angle photo reference spaces

### **7. Animal Wrangling Visual Reference**
Detailed animal handling documentation:
- Sarge (8'6" polar bear) - safety protocols & containment scene
- 100+ rescue birds - housing, care, dialogue training
- Pirate (dog) - training requirements
- Red fox - wild animal protocols
- Safety diagrams, vet schedules, emergency procedures

### **8. VFX & Stunt Visual Reference**
Action sequence breakdowns:
- Polar bear containment (stunt choreography, safety zones)
- Bird observatory fight (breakaway props, bird safety)
- High-speed U-turn (vehicle stunt, camera car)
- Cliffside shooting (weapons, safety harnesses, cliff edge protocols)
- Storyboard spaces, camera diagrams, VFX shot lists

### **9. Location Scout Visual Reference**
Comprehensive location documentation:
- All 8+ locations with multi-angle photo spaces
- Technical specs (dimensions, power, access)
- Site maps and diagrams
- Permits, fees, insurance requirements
- Safety assessments (especially cliff locations)
- Logistics (parking, basecamp, weather)

## **How to Use These Templates:**

Each template includes:
- ✅ **Fillable fields** for all key information
- ✅ **Photo reference spaces** for visual documentation
- ✅ **Tracking charts** for continuity and scheduling
- ✅ **Safety protocols** where applicable
- ✅ **Interactive HTML versions** (links provided) for digital workflow
- ✅ **Print-ready formats** for production binders

You now have a **complete visual pre-production system** covering every department from cinematography to animal wrangling!

</suggestion>

</suggestion>
